data.content: The bug is clear — `ProcessPayment` only checks for zero, not negative amounts. The fastest workflow is **`dotnet watch test`**, which auto-reruns tests on every file save. Let me set it up:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The test expects an `ArgumentException` for negative amounts.

The fastest workflow is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests on every file save. Let me set that up and also fix the bug.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch for the failing test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-dc3b5a11e48d414f87d2e02f60dfc455 && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch for the failing test

data.content: Missing `using System;`. Let me fix both that and the actual bug:

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts

data.content: Tests passed! Let me stop the watcher and verify the final file:

All tests pass. Here's the summary:

**The fix:** Changed `amount == 0` to `amount <= 0` so negative amounts are also rejected (line 30). Also added `using System;` for `ArgumentException`.

**The fast workflow:** Use `dotnet watch test` — it watches for file changes and **automatically re-runs tests on save**, no manual rebuild needed. You can filter to a single test with `--filter`:

```
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

This gives you a tight edit → save → see results loop without leaving your terminal.
